attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- commit: Stage and commit changes with a conventional commit message
- improve-codebase-architecture: Find deepening opportunities in a codebase, informed by the domain language in CONTEXT.md and the decisions in docs/adr/. Use when the user wants to improve architecture, find refactoring opportunities, consolidate tightly-coupled modules, or make a codebase more testable and AI-navigable.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- triage: Triage issues through a state machine driven by triage roles. Use when user wants to create an issue, triage issues, review incoming bugs or feature requests, prepare issues for an AFK agent, or manage issue workflow.
- prototype: Build a throwaway prototype to flush out a design before committing to it. Routes between two branches — a runnable terminal app for state/business-logic questions, or several radically different UI variations toggleable from one route. Use when the user wants to prototype, sanity-check a data model or state machine, mock up a UI, explore design options, or says "prototype this", "let me play with it", "try a few designs".
- diagnose: Disciplined diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Reproduce → minimise → hypothesise → instrument → fix → regression-test. Use when user says "diagnose this" / "debug this", reports a bug, says something is broken/throwing/failing, or describes a performance regression.
- to-issues: Break a plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable issues on the project issue tracker using tracer-bullet vertical slices. Use when user wants to convert a plan into issues, create implementation tickets, or break down work into issues.
- write-a-skill: Create new agent skills with proper structure, progressive disclosure, and bundled resources. Use when user wants to create, write, or build a new skill.
- grill-with-docs: Grilling session that challenges your plan against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates documentation (CONTEXT.md, ADRs) inline as decisions crystallise. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan against their project's language and documented decisions.
- caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by dropping filler, articles, and pleasantries while keeping full technical accuracy. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman.
- tdd: Test-driven development with red-green-refactor loop. Use when user wants to build features or fix bugs using TDD, mentions "red-green-refactor", wants integration tests, or asks for test-first development.
- grill-me: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan, get grilled on their design, or mentions "grill me".
- to-prd: Turn the current conversation context into a PRD and publish it to the project issue tracker. Use when user wants to create a PRD from the current context.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

I appreciate your request, but I'm unable to extract the HTML, CSS, and structure from the provided content. The web page data appears to be **corrupted or binary-encoded** rather than human-readable HTML markup.

What you've shared looks like compressed, encrypted, or garbled data—possibly due to:
- Gzip compression that wasn't properly decompressed
- Binary encoding issues
- Corrupted file transmission
- Character set conversion problems

**To help you effectively, I would need:**

1. **Plain text HTML source** - You can obtain this by:
   - Right-clicking a webpage → "View Page Source" → copy the raw HTML
   - Using browser developer tools (F12) → Elements tab
   - Saving the page as HTML and opening in a text editor

2. **A specific URL** - I could potentially help describe typical extension detail page layouts (header with install button, sidebar navigation, README section, version/compatibility info, screenshots)

3. **A clarification** - Are you trying to:
   - Reverse-engineer a browser extension store design?
   - Recreate a similar layout for your own project?
   - Understand standard patterns for extension marketplaces?

If you can provide clean, readable HTML or clarify your specific design goal, I'd be happy to help with layout structure, typography, spacing, and component organization.

1966 // ─── Detail Page ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 1967 function DetailPage({ ext, lang, t, theme, isDark, cardRadius, cardGap, density, installed, saved, activeTab, setActiveTab, onBack, onInstall, onAdd, onOpenExt, onTagClick, allExtensions }) { 1968 const isInstalled = installed.has(ext.id); 1969 const isSaved = saved.has(ext.id); 1970 const [copied, setCopied] = React.useState(false); 1971 1972 const formatNum = (n) => n >= 1000 ? `${(n/1000).toFixed(n >= 100000 ? 0 : 1)}k` : n; 1973 const installCmd = `agent install ${ext.author}/${ext.name.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g,'-')}`; 1974 const copyCmd = () => { 1975 navigator.clipboard?.writeText(installCmd); 1976 setCopied(true); 1977 setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 1400); 1978 }; 1979 1980 // Mock metadata 1981 const meta = { 1982 version: "2.4.1", 1983 updated: "3 days ago", 1984 updatedZh: "3 天前", 1985 size: "184 KB", 1986 license: "MIT", 1987 homepage: `https://${ext.author}.com`, 1988 repo: `github.com/${ext.author}/${ext.name.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g,'-')}`, 1989 requirements: ext.category === "mcp" ? 1	import { count, desc, eq, sql } from "drizzle-orm";
2	
3	import { db } from "@/lib/db/client";
4	import { extensions, extensionTags } from "@/lib/db/schema";
5	import {
6	  buildExtensionOrder,
7	  buildExtensionWhere,
8	} from "@/lib/search/query";
9	import {
10	  PAGE_SIZE,
11	  pageOffset,
12	  type Filters,
13	} from "@/lib/validators/filters";
14	
15	const listSelect = {
16	  id: extensions.id,
17	  slug: extensions.slug,
18	  category: extensions.category,
19	  badge: extensions.badge,
20	  scope: extensions.scope,
21	  funcCat: extensions.funcCat,
22	  subCat: extensions.subCat,
23	  l2: extensions.l2,
24	  deptId: extensions.deptId,
25	  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
26	  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
27	  name: extensions.name,
28	  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
29	  description: extensions.description,
30	  descriptionZh: extensions.descriptionZh,
31	  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
32	  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
33	  // array_agg returns null when the LEFT JOIN finds no tags; coalesce to '{}'.
34	  tagIds: sql<string[]>`coalesce(array_agg(${extensionTags.tagId}) FILTER (WHERE ${extensionTags.tagId} IS NOT NULL), '{}')`,
35	};
36	
37	export async function listExtensions(
38	  filters: Filters,
39	  userDeptId?: string,
40	) {
41	  const where = buildExtensionWhere(filters, userDeptId);
42	  const order = buildExtensionOrder(filters.sort);
43	
44	  return db
45	    .select(listSelect)
46	    .from(extensions)
47	    .leftJoin(
48	      extensionTags,
49	      eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id),
50	    )
51	    .where(where)
52	    .groupBy(extensions.id)
53	    .orderBy(...order)
54	    .limit(PAGE_SIZE)
55	    .offset(pageOffset(filters.page));
56	}
57	
58	export type ExtensionListItem = Awaited<
59	  ReturnType<typeof listExtensions>
60	>[number];
61	
62	export async function countFilteredExtensions(
63	  filters: Filters,
64	  userDeptId?: string,
65	) {
66	  const where = buildExtensionWhere(filters, userDeptId);
67	  const [row] = await db
68	    .select({ count: count() })
69	    .from(extensions)
70	    .where(where);
71	  return Number(row?.count ?? 0);
72	}
73	
74	export async function getFeaturedExtension() {
75	  const [row] = await db
76	    .select({
77	      id: extensions.id,
78	      slug: extensions.slug,
79	      name: extensions.name,
80	      nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
81	      description: extensions.description,
82	      descriptionZh: extensions.descriptionZh,
83	      iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
84	      iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
85	      downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
86	    })
87	    .from(extensions)
88	    .where(eq(extensions.badge, "official"))
89	    .orderBy(desc(extensions.downloadsCount))
90	    .limit(1);
91	  return row ?? null;
92	}
93	
94	export async function getExtensionBySlug(slug: string) {
95	  const [row] = await db
96	    .select({
97	      id: extensions.id,
98	      slug: extensions.slug,
99	      category: extensions.category,
100	      badge: extensions.badge,
101	      scope: extensions.scope,
102	      funcCat: extensions.funcCat,
103	      subCat: extensions.subCat,
104	      l2: extensions.l2,
105	      deptId: extensions.deptId,
106	      iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
107	      iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
108	      name: extensions.name,
109	      nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
110	      tagline: extensions.tagline,
111	      taglineZh: extensions.taglineZh,
112	      description: extensions.description,
113	      descriptionZh: extensions.descriptionZh,
114	      readmeMd: extensions.readmeMd,
115	      homepageUrl: extensions.homepageUrl,
116	      repoUrl: extensions.repoUrl,
117	      licenseSpdx: extensions.licenseSpdx,
118	      compatibilityJson: extensions.compatibilityJson,
119	      downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
120	      starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
121	      ratingsCount: extensions.ratingsCount,
122	      publishedAt: extensions.publishedAt,
123	      tagIds: sql<string[]>`coalesce(array_agg(${extensionTags.tagId}) FILTER (WHERE ${extensionTags.tagId} IS NOT NULL), '{}')`,
124	    })
125	    .from(extensions)
126	    .leftJoin(
127	      extensionTags,
128	      eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id),
129	    )
130	    .where(eq(extensions.slug, slug))
131	    .groupBy(extensions.id)
132	    .limit(1);
133	  return row ?? null;
134	}
135	
136	export type ExtensionDetail = NonNullable<
137	  Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getExtensionBySlug>>
138	>;
139	
140	export async function countPublishedExtensions() {
141	  const [row] = await db
142	    .select({ count: count() })
143	    .from(extensions)
144	    .where(eq(extensions.visibility, "published"));
145	  return Number(row?.count ?? 0);
146	}
147

ownerOrgId: text()
      .notNull()
      .references(() => organizations.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    deptId: text().references(() => departments.id, { onDelete: "set null" }),
    iconEmoji: text(),
    iconColor: text(),
    visibility: visibilityEnum().notNull().default("draft"),
    // i18n columns
    name: text().notNull(),
    nameZh: text(),
    tagline: text(),
    taglineZh: text(),
    description: text(),
    descriptionZh: text(),
    // Long-form markdown body for the detail page. Not duplicated to ZH yet —
    // most extensions will have an English README, with translations layered
    // on later if needed.
    readmeMd: text(),
    // metadata for detail page sidebar
    homepageUrl: text(),
    repoUrl: text(),
    licenseSpdx: text(),
    compatibilityJson: jsonb(),
    // denormalized counters (updated by jobs)
    downloadsCount: integer().notNull().default(0),
    starsAvg: numeric({ precision: 2, scale: 1 }).notNull().default("0.0"),
    ratingsCount: integer().notNull().default(0),
    publishedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }),
    createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
    updatedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
    searchVector: tsvector("search_vector"),
  },
  (t) => [
    index("idx_ext_category").on(t.category),
    index("idx_ext_scope").on(t.scope),
    index("idx_ext_func_sub_l2").on(t.funcCat, t.subCat, t.l2),
    index("idx_ext_dept_path").using(
      "btree",
      sql`${t.deptId} text_pattern_ops`,
    ),
    index("idx_ext_visibility").on(t.visibility),
    index("idx_ext_downloads").on(sql`${t.downloadsCount} DESC`),
    index("idx_ext_stars").on(sql`${t.starsAvg} DESC`),
  ],
);

export const extensionVersions = pgTable(
  "extension_versions",
  {
    id: text().primaryKey(),
    extensionId: text()
      .notNull()
      .references(() => extensions.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    version: text().notNull(), // semver
    changelog: text(),
    changelogZh: text(),
    manifestJson: jsonb(),
    // Logical reference to files.id; no FK constraint to avoid a circular FK
    // with files.extensionVersionId. App code keeps these consistent.
    bundleFileId: text(),
    status: versionStatusEnum().notNull().default("pending"),
    publishedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }),
    createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
  },
  (t) => [unique("ext_version_unique").on(t.extensionId, t.version)],
);

export const files = pgTable("files", {
  id: text().primaryKey(),
  extensionVersionId: text().references(() => extensionVersions.id, {
    onDelete: "set null",
  }),
  r2Key: text().notNull(),
  size: bigint({ mode: "bigint" }).notNull(),
  checksumSha256: text().notNull(),

12	  text,
13	  timestamp,
14	  unique,
15	} from "drizzle-orm/pg-core";
16	
17	import { users } from "./auth";
18	import { departments, organizations } from "./org";
19	
20	// tsvector is a Postgres-native type; Drizzle doesn't ship a built-in helper.
21	// We declare it as a custom type so the ORM can reference it in WHERE clauses
22	// via sql``. It is GENERATED ALWAYS (read-only); never written by app code.
23	const tsvector = customType<{ data: string }>({
24	  dataType() { return "tsvector"; },
25	});
26	
27	export const extensionCategoryEnum = pgEnum("extension_category", [
28	  "skills",
29	  "mcp",
30	  "slash",
31	  "plugins",
32	]);
33	
34	export const extensionScopeEnum = pgEnum("extension_scope", [
35	  "personal",
36	  "org",
37	  "enterprise",
38	]);
39	
40	export const extensionBadgeEnum = pgEnum("extension_badge", [
41	  "official",
42	  "popular",
43	  "new",
44	]);
45	
46	export const funcCatEnum = pgEnum("func_cat", [
47	  "workTask",
48	  "business",
49	  "tools",
50	]);
51	
52	export const visibilityEnum = pgEnum("extension_visibility", [
53	  "draft",
54	  "published",
55	  "archived",
56	]);
57	
58	export const versionStatusEnum = pgEnum("version_status", [
59	  "pending",
60	  "scanning",
61	  "ready",
62	  "rejected",
63	]);
64	
65	export const fileScanStatusEnum = pgEnum("file_scan_status", [
66	  "pending",
67	  "clean",
68	  "flagged",
69	]);
70	
71	export const extensions = pgTable(
72	  "extensions",
73	  {
74	    id: text().primaryKey(),
75	    slug: text().notNull().unique(),
76	    category: extensionCategoryEnum().notNull(),
77	    badge: extensionBadgeEnum(),
78	    scope: extensionScopeEnum().notNull(),
79	    funcCat: funcCatEnum().notNull(),
80	    subCat: text().notNull(),
81	    l2: text(),
82	    publisherUserId: text().references(() => users.id, {
83	      onDelete: "set null",
84	    }),
85	    ownerOrgId: text()

150	);
151	
152	export const files = pgTable("files", {
153	  id: text().primaryKey(),
154	  extensionVersionId: text().references(() => extensionVersions.id, {
155	    onDelete: "set null",
156	  }),
157	  r2Key: text().notNull(),
158	  size: bigint({ mode: "bigint" }).notNull(),
159	  checksumSha256: text().notNull(),
160	  mimeType: text(),
161	  scanStatus: fileScanStatusEnum().notNull().default("pending"),
162	  scanReport: jsonb(),
163	  createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
164	});
165	
166	export const tags = pgTable("tags", {
167	  id: text().primaryKey(), // tag key, e.g. "real-time"
168	  labelEn: text().notNull(),
169	  labelZh: text().notNull(),
170	});
171	
172	export const extensionTags = pgTable(
173	  "extension_tags",
174	  {
175	    extensionId: text()
176	      .notNull()
177	      .references(() => extensions.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
178	    tagId: text()
179	      .notNull()
180	      .references(() => tags.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
181	  },
182	  (t) => [
183	    primaryKey({ columns: [t.extensionId, t.tagId] }),
184	    index("idx_extension_tags_tag_ext").on(t.tagId, t.extensionId),
185	  ],
186	);

A few options, in rough priority order:

1. **Land the work** — these changes are uncommitted on `main`, but per `CLAUDE.md` we always work on a feature branch + PR. I can spin up `feat/detail-page-redesign`, commit, push, and open the PR for review.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/hutusi/.claude/skills/tdd

# Test-Driven Development

## Philosophy

**Core principle**: Tests should verify behavior through public interfaces, not implementation details. Code can change entirely; tests shouldn't.

**Good tests** are integration-style: they exercise real code paths through public APIs. They describe _what_ the system does, not _how_ it does it. A good test reads like a specification - "user can checkout with valid cart" tells you exactly what capability exists. These tests survive refactors because they don't care about internal structure.

**Bad tests** are coupled to implementation. They mock internal collaborators, test private methods, or verify through external means (like querying a database directly instead of using the interface). The warning sign: your test breaks when you refactor, but behavior hasn't changed. If you rename an internal function and tests fail, those tests were testing implementation, not behavior.

See [tests.md](tests.md) for examples and [mocking.md](mocking.md) for mocking guidelines.

## Anti-Pattern: Horizontal Slices

**DO NOT write all tests first, then all implementation.** This is "horizontal slicing" - treating RED as "write all tests" and GREEN as "write all code."

This produces **crap tests**:

- Tests written in bulk test _imagined_ behavior, not _actual_ behavior
- You end up testing the _shape_ of things (data structures, function signatures) rather than user-facing behavior
- Tests become insensitive to real changes - they pass when behavior breaks, fail when behavior is fine
- You outrun your headlights, committing to test structure before understanding the implementation

**Correct approach**: Vertical slices via tracer bullets. One test → one implementation → repeat. Each test responds to what you learned from the previous cycle. Because you just wrote the code, you know exactly what behavior matters and how to verify it.

```
WRONG (horizontal):
  RED:   test1, test2, test3, test4, test5
  GREEN: impl1, impl2, impl3, impl4, impl5

RIGHT (vertical):
  RED→GREEN: test1→impl1
  RED→GREEN: test2→impl2
  RED→GREEN: test3→impl3
  ...
```

## Workflow

### 1. Planning

When exploring the codebase, use the project's domain glossary so that test names and interface vocabulary match the project's language, and respect ADRs in the area you're touching.

Before writing any code:

- [ ] Confirm with user what interface changes are needed
- [ ] Confirm with user which behaviors to test (prioritize)
- [ ] Identify opportunities for [deep modules](deep-modules.md) (small interface, deep implementation)
- [ ] Design interfaces for [testability](interface-design.md)
- [ ] List the behaviors to test (not implementation steps)
- [ ] Get user approval on the plan

Ask: "What should the public interface look like? Which behaviors are most important to test?"

**You can't test everything.** Confirm with the user exactly which behaviors matter most. Focus testing effort on critical paths and complex logic, not every possible edge case.

### 2. Tracer Bullet

Write ONE test that confirms ONE thing about the system:

```
RED:   Write test for first behavior → test fails
GREEN: Write minimal code to pass → test passes
```

This is your tracer bullet - proves the path works end-to-end.

### 3. Incremental Loop

For each remaining behavior:

```
RED:   Write next test → fails
GREEN: Minimal code to pass → passes
```

Rules:

- One test at a time
- Only enough code to pass current test
- Don't anticipate future tests
- Keep tests focused on observable behavior

### 4. Refactor

After all tests pass, look for [refactor candidates](refactoring.md):

- [ ] Extract duplication
- [ ] Deepen modules (move complexity behind simple interfaces)
- [ ] Apply SOLID principles where natural
- [ ] Consider what new code reveals about existing code
- [ ] Run tests after each refactor step

**Never refactor while RED.** Get to GREEN first.

## Checklist Per Cycle

```
[ ] Test describes behavior, not implementation
[ ] Test uses public interface only
[ ] Test would survive internal refactor
[ ] Code is minimal for this test
[ ] No speculative features added
```


ARGUMENTS: add tests for this feature
Let me see the existing test setup before planning what to cover.

content.text:
Vitest covers `components/`, `lib/`, `cli/` only — server components and pages go through Playwright e2e (which isn't part of `bun test`). DB queries don't run in unit tests (no real DB connection). That narrows the surface.

Here's what I can meaningfully cover in vitest, prioritized:

**High value — new client behavior**
1. `ExtTabs` — clicking a non-default tab swaps the visible panel; default tab renders first
2. `InstallCommand` — Copy button writes the command to the clipboard and shows "Copied"
3. `ShareButton` — falls back to clipboard when `navigator.share` is undefined; shows "Copied"
4. `SaveButton` pill variant — renders the label text (icon variant already tested)

**Medium — only worth it if you want belt-and-braces**
5. `InstallCommand`/`ShareButton` — "Copied" reverts after the timeout

**Out of scope for vitest in this project**
- Page rendering (404, default tab, Overview content) — Playwright territory
- DB queries (`getLatestExtensionVersion`, etc.) — no DB in unit tests
- Hero/AboutCard/RelatedList rendering — server components; smoke-rendering server components in vitest is fragile and the value is low

My recommendation: do (1)–(4). That's four small files, each one clear behavior. Skip (5) — testing setTimeout/timer behavior tends to be brittle.

OK to proceed with (1)–(4)?

content.text:
191 passing across 21 files (was 182 across 18). Lint, typecheck, all green.

**Diff summary for the test commit:**
- `components/extension/ext-tabs.test.tsx` *(new, 3 tests)* — default tab, click swap, defaultTab prop
- `components/extension/install-command.test.tsx` *(new, 2 tests)* — render, click copies + flips label
- `components/extension/share-button.test.tsx` *(new, 2 tests)* — render, click copies via clipboard fallback
- `components/extension/save-button.test.tsx` — added 2 tests covering the `pill` variant (label visible, flips to "saved")

**Proposed commit message:**

```
test(detail): cover new client components from the detail-page redesign

Adds vitest coverage for the four new/updated client components on
the redesigned extension detail page:

- ExtTabs: default panel, click-to-swap, defaultTab override
- InstallCommand: render and copy-on-click (verifies the user-event
  clipboard contents and the "Copied" label flip)
- ShareButton: same copy-and-flip path through the navigator.share
  fallback
- SaveButton (pill variant): label visibility and post-save state

DB queries and the page itself are unchanged scope — those are
Playwright territory in this repo's vitest config.
```

OK to commit?

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat(detail): redesign extension detail page with tabbed layout" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- Rebuild the extension detail page to match the new `AgentCenter.html` design from Claude Design.
- New hero (large icon, category breadcrumb, badge, verified tag, Install/Save/Share CTA row, four-stat grid) and tabbed body: **Overview** (README + tags), **Setup** (install command + requirements), **Versions** (real changelog), **Reviews** (placeholder).
- Sidebar swaps the metadata panel for an **About** card (publisher / version / size / license / updated / scope) plus homepage/repo links and a **Related extensions** card.
- Adds queries for the latest published version, full version list, and related extensions in the same category. Adds a `detail.*` i18n namespace for both locales.
- Vitest coverage for the four new/updated client components: `ExtTabs`, `InstallCommand`, `ShareButton`, `SaveButton` pill variant (191 tests pass, +9).

## Notes

- The Permissions tab from the design is intentionally dropped — the schema doesn't carry permission data yet. Easy to add back when the manifest spec grows.
- DB queries and server-component rendering are out of scope for this PR's tests; they belong in Playwright per `vitest.config.ts`.

## Test plan

- [ ] `bun run validate` (lint + typecheck + tests) passes
- [ ] `/{en,zh}/extensions/<slug>` renders the new hero, tabs, and sidebar
- [ ] Clicking each tab swaps the body content
- [ ] Setup tab: Copy on the install command writes to the clipboard and shows "Copied"
- [ ] Hero: Share button falls back to clipboard when `navigator.share` is absent
- [ ] Versions tab marks the latest entry as "Current"
- [ ] About card renders homepage + repo links and "—" for missing fields
- [ ] Related card hides when there are no other extensions in the category
EOF
)"

It adds database queries for versions and related extensions, introduces new reusable components (`ExtTabs`, `ExtAboutCard`, `ExtRelatedList`, `InstallCommand`, `ShareButton`), updates `ExtHero` and `SaveButton`, removes the deprecated `ExtMetadataPanel`, and adds comprehensive test coverage and i18n support. ## Changes **Extension Detail Page Refactor** | Layer / File(s) | Summary | |---|---| | **Database Queries**
`lib/db/queries/extensions.ts` | Added `getLatestExtensionVersion`, `listExtensionVersions`, and `getRelatedExtensions` to fetch version history and related extensions from the database. | | **Type Contracts**
`components/extension/*.tsx` | Defined prop interfaces for new components (`ExtAboutCardProps`, `ExtRelatedListProps`) and extended `ExtHeroProps` with version, published date, and localization label fields. | | **Action Buttons**

{"body":"_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Clipboard is written (and label flips to "Copied") when the user cancels the native share sheet.**

On devices where `navigator.share` is available (mobile Chrome/Safari), dismissing the share dialog throws `AbortError`. The current `catch` block silently falls through to `clipboard.writeText`, so the user's clipboard is modified and the button shows "Copied" even though they deliberately opted out of sharing.

The cleanest fix is to only use the clipboard fallback when `navigator.share` is genuinely absent:

<summary>🐛 Proposed fix","line":31,"path":"components/extension/share-button.tsx","user":"coderabbitai[bot]"}

content.text:
Here's the full CodeRabbit triage for PR #12 — 6 inline comments:

| # | File:Line | Severity | Issue |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `page.tsx:67` | Major | `x-forwarded-host`/`host` headers are spoofable → share URL could point anywhere. Use a trusted public base URL or host allowlist. |
| 2 | `ext-about-card.tsx:112` | Major | `homepageUrl`/`repoUrl` come from extension manifests → should validate the URL parses and is `http:`/`https:` before rendering in `href`, else `javascript:` URIs sneak through. |
| 3 | `ext-tabs.tsx:49` | Major | The manual `role="tab"` markup is click-only, no keyboard nav (arrow/Home/End), no `aria-controls`. Project already uses `@base-ui/react` (see `select.tsx`). Suggests using `@base-ui/react/tabs`. |
| 4 | `install-command.tsx:22` | Major | `await navigator.clipboard?.writeText(...)` evaluates to `await undefined` if clipboard is missing, which resolves successfully — so we flip to "Copied" without actually copying. Guard before awaiting. |
| 5 | `install-command.tsx:41` | Major | Hard-coded zinc/hex values for the terminal block. Suggests replacing with theme tokens. |
| 6 | `share-button.tsx:31` | Minor | If user cancels the native share sheet on mobile (`AbortError`), the `catch` falls through to `clipboard.writeText` and shows "Copied" — user opted out of sharing but their clipboard was overwritten anyway. |

**My take on each:**

- **(1), (2), (4)** are real correctness/security bugs worth fixing — small changes.
- **(6)** is a real UX bug, also small.
- **(3)** is legitimate accessibility work; modest effort, fits the project's Base UI pattern.
- **(5)** I'd push back: the dark terminal block is intentional (the original `AgentCenter.html` design also uses dark in both themes — terminals are conventionally dark even in light UIs). Worth a reply explaining why we're keeping it.

Recommend fixing 1, 2, 4, 6, and 3, and replying to dismiss 5. Want me to do that?

content.text:
**Proposed commit:**

```
fix(detail): address CodeRabbit security and a11y feedback on PR #12

- Build the share URL from a trusted NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL instead of
  request headers (x-forwarded-host is spoofable when the edge layer
  doesn't rewrite it).
- Allowlist http(s) on extension-supplied homepage/repo URLs before
  rendering them in href via a new safeExternalUrl helper, so
  javascript:/data: URIs can't reach the DOM.
- Replace the hand-rolled tablist in ExtTabs with @base-ui/react/tabs
  to get keyboard navigation (arrow / Home / End), aria-controls
  wiring, and selected-state styling for free. Public API of ExtTabs
  is unchanged.
- Guard navigator.clipboard?.writeText before awaiting it in
  InstallCommand — `await undefined` was resolving successfully and
  flipping the label to "Copied" without an actual write.
- Stop falling through to clipboard.writeText when navigator.share()
  rejects in ShareButton: an AbortError on mobile means the user
  cancelled the share sheet, not that the API is missing.

Tests: keep ExtTabs coverage and add an ARIA-wiring assertion
(aria-selected, aria-controls, data-orientation). The arrow-key
nav itself is Base UI's contract; happy-dom can't simulate the
focus-key chain it depends on, so we don't try to reproduce it
in vitest.
```

OK to commit?

fix(detail): address CodeRabbit security and a11y feedback on PR #12

- Build the share URL from a trusted NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL instead of
  request headers (x-forwarded-host is spoofable when the edge layer
  doesn't rewrite it).
- Allowlist http(s) on extension-supplied homepage/repo URLs before
  rendering them in href via a new safeExternalUrl helper, so
  javascript:/data: URIs can't reach the DOM.
- Replace the hand-rolled tablist in ExtTabs with @base-ui/react/tabs
  to get keyboard navigation (arrow / Home / End), aria-controls
  wiring, and selected-state styling for free. Public API of ExtTabs
  is unchanged.
- Guard navigator.clipboard?.writeText before awaiting it in
  InstallCommand — `await undefined` was resolving successfully and
  flipping the label to "Copied" without an actual write.
- Stop falling through to clipboard.writeText when navigator.share()
  rejects in ShareButton: an AbortError on mobile means the user
  cancelled the share sheet, not that the API is missing.

Tests: keep ExtTabs coverage and add an ARIA-wiring assertion
(aria-selected, aria-controls, data-orientation). The arrow-key
nav itself is Base UI's contract; happy-dom can't simulate the
focus-key chain it depends on, so we don't try to reproduce it
in vitest.
EOF
)" && git status
